events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We are asked to output JSON only. The scenario: equipment bonding, residential, in-progress. Observed condition: "isolated metal parts". That likely refers to metal parts that are not bonded to the grounding system. In equipment bonding, isolated metal parts that are likely to become energized must be bonded. NEC 250.104 covers bonding of piping and exposed structural metal. 250.30 covers grounding separately derived systems. Isolated metal parts could be enclosures, conduits, etc. that are not grounded, creating shock hazard. So this is a fail, risk high or critical. Immediate controls: stop work, bond parts.

So decision: fail. Risk: high or critical? likely high. Work stage: in-progress. Component conditions: installed-defective? non-compliant? Both? The condition is that metal parts are isolated, i.e., not bonded, which is non-compliant. Might also be defective installation if bonding was omitted. So "non-compliant" and maybe "installed-defective". But "installed-defective" implies it was installed but defective. Could be that bonding was never done. I think "non-compliant" applies. Use both? Possibly. We'll include "non-compliant". Percent complete: hard to say, maybe 70%? Not critical. Value: none. Sound source: not asked. Confidence: 0.9. Event time, rate: not needed. Order: not needed. Workable: probably none because hazard needs correction. findings: "Isolated metal parts not bonded to the grounding system, posing shock hazard." actions: "Immediately bond isolated metal parts per NEC 250.104." rationale: brief. references: ["NEC 250.104", "250.30"].

Output JSON.

    "Stop work immediately. Bond all isolated metal parts to the equipment grounding conductor per NEC 250.104."
  ],
  "rationale": "Ungrounded metal parts can become energized from a ground fault, posing a shock risk. NEC 250.104 requires bonding of metal parts likely to become energized.",
  "references": ["NEC 250.104", "NEC 250.30"]
}
